# Stack Checklist

Review items derived from the grove technology stack and testing approach. Use this checklist when planning changes to ensure stack-specific concerns are addressed.

## Rust Language & Build

- [ ] **Edition**: Rust 2021 edition — ensure `Cargo.toml` specifies `edition = "2021"`
- [ ] **Clippy**: All changes pass `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` with no warnings
- [ ] **Release build**: `cargo build --release --locked` succeeds
- [ ] **LTO**: Release profile uses `lto = "thin"` and `strip = true` — verify binary size is not regressively large
- [ ] **No unsafe**: Project uses no `unsafe` blocks — keep it that way unless required by tree-sitter FFI

## Dependencies

- [ ] **tree-sitter 0.25**: Verify WASM feature is enabled (`features = ["wasm"]`). Grammar WASM modules must be `dylink.0` (native WASM)
- [ ] **clap 4**: Derive macro usage — ensure `#[derive(Parser)]` and `#[derive(Subcommand)]` are correct
- [ ] **serde/serde_json**: All JSON output structs derive `Serialize`. MCP serialization must not use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if)]` inconsistently
- [ ] **ureq (rustls)**: HTTP client uses rustls (not native TLS) — no OpenSSL dependency
- [ ] **sha2**: Content hashes must use `sha256:` prefix format (not bare hex)
- [ ] **ignore**: Directory walks must respect `.gitignore` — verify new walk calls use `WalkBuilder`

## Grammar System

- [ ] **WASM loading**: Each grammar is loaded once per process (OnceLock). Verify no grammar leaks between tests
- [ ] **Registry search order**: GROVE_REGISTRY env → project `.grove/grammars` → OS cache → dev tree. Changes must not break this precedence
- [ ] **Lockfile integrity**: `grove.lock` must be deterministic (sorted) and include sha256 hashes. Test with `write_lock_for`
- [ ] **New grammar addition**: Requires manifest.json + grammar.wasm + tags.scm in `registry/<lang>/`. Optionally locals.scm and imports.scm
- [ ] **Manifest schema**: Must include `name`, `version`, `extensions[]`, and `profile` (with defaults)

## CLI Surface

- [ ] **JSON output**: `--json` global flag must produce valid JSON for every subcommand
- [ ] **Human output**: Default output goes to stdout, diagnostics to stderr
- [ ] **Exit codes**: `check` exits 1 on syntax errors; all other commands exit 0 on success, 1 on error
- [ ] **Symbol IDs**: Format `<lang>:<relpath>#<name>@<line>` — 1-based line numbers, stable across tools

## MCP Server

- [ ] **Protocol compatibility**: Must support `2025-06-18`, `2025-03-26`, `2024-11-05`. New changes must not break older clients
- [ ] **Stdio transport**: All protocol output on stdout, all diagnostics on stderr. Never mix them
- [ ] **Tool schemas**: Must be plain `{type: object, properties, required?}` — no top-level `anyOf`/`oneOf`
- [ ] **Error handling**: Tool errors return `isError: true` result, not JSON-RPC errors
- [ ] **Notifications**: `notifications/initialized` and `notifications/cancelled` must produce no response

## Testing

- [ ] **Unit tests**: Inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in each module — no separate test module files
- [ ] **Integration tests**: `tests/cli.rs` — smoke tests for CLI subcommands
- [ ] **Test command**: `cargo test --release --locked` — all tests must pass at release optimization
- [ ] **Grammar tests**: Registry module has unit tests for index building, lock writing, manifest parsing
- [ ] **Profile defaults**: `Profile::default()` must work (empty `call_kinds` defaults to `["call"]`)

## Cross-Platform

- [ ] **Binary packaging**: Unix → `.tar.gz`, Windows → `.zip`. Verify sha256 checksums in both
- [ ] **Cache directories**: Use `dirs` crate for OS-native paths — never hardcode `~/.cache`
- [ ] **Path handling**: All path joins use `Path::join` — never string concatenation

## Distribution

- [ ] **npm wrapper**: `dist/npm/` must reference correct version and binary name
- [ ] **Homebrew formula**: `dist/homebrew/grove.rb` must match release tag and sha256
- [ ] **GitHub Release**: 5 targets, all with checksums. Tag format: `v*.*.*`
